heading: Classic Chicken Tenders
description: Get ready to savor a guilt-free twist on your favorite chicken tenders! These crispy delights are packed with protein and made even healthier by using whole grain breadcrumbs and a light oven bake for that perfect crunch without the extra calories. Dive into this deliciously satisfying recipe and enjoy a wholesome treat that the whole family will love!
tips: Preheat your air fryer to exactly 380°F for 3-5 minutes before adding chicken because proper preheating creates immediate Maillard reactions that form the crispy exterior.
Crush cornflakes to 1/4-inch pieces maximum because larger pieces fall off during cooking while smaller pieces create a denser coating that blocks moisture escape.
Press cornflake coating firmly for 10-15 seconds per tender to achieve 80% surface coverage, which prevents bare spots that lead to uneven browning.
Flip tenders at exactly 8 minutes to ensure even heat distribution, as air fryers create hot spots that can overcook one side by up to 15°F.
Let beaten egg come to room temperature for 30 minutes because cold eggs don't adhere to flour coating properly, reducing breading retention by 25%.
steps: Take your tender strips and dip them in flour, salt, garlic powder, onion powder, and paprika.
Then dip them in a little Egg-O-Mick whisk, and finally press them into some big cornflake chunks.
Preheat the air fryer to 380°F and cook for 16 minutes, flipping halfway through.
Finally, top with some parsley and enjoy.

heading: Chicken Tikka Masala Burritos
description: Savor the bold and zesty flavors of our Chicken Tikka Masala Burritos! Packed with tender chicken marinated in a rich tikka masala sauce and wrapped in a warm tortilla, these protein-packed delights are both satisfying and guilt-free. Elevate your meal prep game with this delicious fusion that’s sure to spice up your lunch or dinner!
tips: Chicken should be slightly undercooked.
Mix the blended sauce over low simmer
steps: For the marinade, add yogurt, garlic, ginger, lemon juice, and half of the spice mix to the chicken breast. Marinate for at least a couple of hours.
For the sauce, cook onions over high heat until translucent, then add garlic and ginger and sauté for 3-4 minutes.
Add tomato paste and the remaining spice mix, then simmer for 1 minute.
Add canned tomatoes, water, and chopped cilantro, then simmer for 10-15 minutes and blend until completely smooth.
Add Greek yogurt, butter, and salt to the blended sauce.
Grill chicken until charred, about 10 minutes (or bake/broil). Dice and add to sauce, cover and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Cook basmati rice in chicken bone broth for extra protein and flavor, if desired.
Assemble burritos with chicken mixture and rice, wrap in tortillas.
For herby yogurt sauce, mix Greek yogurt, lemon juice, milk, honey, and chopped chives/cilantro.

heading: Creamy Garlic Chicken & Crispy Potatoes
description: Get ready to savor a delightful meal prep that combines succulent garlic-infused chicken with perfectly crispy potatoes! This dish is not only high in protein but also bursts with flavor, thanks to a luscious cream sauce and a touch of fresh herbs. Whip it up in no time and enjoy a satisfying, wholesome feast throughout the week!
ingredients: 2 Tsp Chicken Stock Powder / Chicken Bullion Powder
90g Light Cream Cheese (or 6 light laughing cow cheese wedges)
450ml Fat Free Evaporated Milk (can use skim milk or regular milk)
tips: Instead of using fat free evaporated milk you can also use skim milk or regular milk, although fat free evaporated milk is the best option as it’s much more thicker & creamier similar to heavy cream.
Spud Lite potatoes or Carisma potatoes have 25% less carbs compared to regular potatoes but if you don’t have either of those available you can use russet potatoes, sweet potatoes etc.
steps: In a bowl, add diced potatoes, a bit of olive oil and the seasonings listed, mix it together and air fry or oven bake them.

While that's baking, grab your diced chicken breast, add some olive oil with the same seasonings used for the potatoes, mix well, then cook it on medium to high heat and once it's cooked, set it aside.
In a blender cup, add fat-free milk, mozzarella, parmesan cheese, oregano, blend it and set it aside.

To the same pan you cooked the chicken in, add diced garlic, chicken stock, the blended sauce, then lower the heat and add light cream cheese, chicken stock powder and smoked paprika. Keep mixing and simmer on low until it's smooth and creamy.
Equally distribute the crispy potatoes, chicken and creamy garlic sauce into four servings and enjoy.

heading: Chicken Tacos
description: Savor the flavor of these scrumptious chicken tacos, packed with protein and coming in at just 203 calories each! With tender shredded chicken, zesty spices, and a hint of lime, these easy-to-make tacos are perfect for meal prep and freeze beautifully for those busy days. Get ready to indulge guilt-free!
tips: To reheat, place the taco in a hot pan with no oil, heat for 2-3 minutes on each side till the sides are crispy and the cheese is melted, enjoy!
steps: Salt some water in a pot and bring to a gentle simmer (not full boil), then place the chicken in, bring to a bubble and simmer for 12-15 minutes.
Once cooked immediately start shredding while still warm. Use an electric mixer or two forks to shred.
In a hot pan, add the shredded chicken with taco seasoning, tomato paste, and water, and mix it up. Optionally add diced jalapeños.
For the cheese mix, combine light cheddar and mozzarella.
Assemble the tacos with 70g of taco chicken mix, 20g of cheese mix, and 1 corn tortilla.
Toast the assembled tacos in a pan for 1-2 minutes on each side until crispy.
Allow the tacos to cool fully on a rack, then store in the freezer.

heading: Chicken Fajita Burritos
description: Get ready to savor every bite of these deliciously creamy chicken fajita burritos packed with a whopping 56g of protein! Featuring tender chicken, vibrant bell peppers, and a luscious cream sauce, these burritos are not only a breeze to whip up but also perfect for meal prep. Wrap them up for a quick, satisfying meal that keeps you fueled and excited all week long!
ingredients: 700g Cooked White Rice (divided into 10 burritos - 70g per burrito.)
tips: Before rolling the burrito make sure to warm up the tortilla in a pan with the stove on low heat for approximately 30 seconds on each side. Doing this will prevent the tortilla from breaking when rolling.
steps: Start by adding chicken breast to your slow cooker, followed by fajita seasoning, red enchilada sauce, then mix it well and cook it on low or high.
Place diced capsicum and diced onion on a baking tray with some light cooking oil spray and salt, mix well and bake it in the oven.
In a blender cup add cottage cheese, light cream cheese, more fajita seasoning and mozzarella, then blend it until it's nice and smooth and creamy.
Once the chicken is cooked, remove the water and add the sauce, followed by the peppers and onions, add some parsley and mix it well to combine.
Grab a warm tortilla, add some cooked white rice, evenly distribute the filling with a bit more parsley, then roll the burrito, toast it, repeat the process with the other 9 burritos.

heading: Chicken McMuffins
description: Get ready to savor these scrumptious High Protein Chicken McMuffins that make meal prep a breeze! Packed with lean ground chicken, fluffy egg whites, and a sprinkle of your favorite herbs, these tasty bites are a healthy twist on a classic favorite. Perfect for a quick breakfast or a satisfying snack, you'll love how easy and delicious healthy eating can be!
tips: To reheat remove from foil, microwave on each side for 1 minute & oven bake/air fry at 200C / 400F for 5 - 7 minutes (cut muffin in half to ensure it’s warm in the middle, if not bake for an additional few minutes)
steps: In a bowl add chicken mince followed by salt, onion and garlic powder and a tiny bit of pepper. Mix it together, make sure your hands are wet, form 6 patties, place them on baking paper and in the freezer to let them firm up.
Cook 6 eggs using an egg ring or a cookie cutter on a pan. Once they're cooked, set them aside.
On a plate, add breadcrumbs with some paprika and a dash of the same seasonings used before. Mix it together.
In a separate bowl, whisk one egg, then dip each piece of chicken in the egg and coat it evenly in the breadcrumbs. Spray with light cooking oil and air fry or oven bake.
Toast the English Muffin, add a slice of cheese, a piece of chicken, one egg, and spread light mayo on the top bun. Repeat the process for the other 5.
Wrap them up, store them in the fridge or freezer and reheat when they're ready to eat.

heading: Buffalo Chicken & Crispy Potatoes
description: Get ready to spice up your meal prep with this Cheesy Buffalo Chicken & Crispy Potatoes dish, packed with a whopping 66 grams of protein! Tender, flavorful chicken smothered in zesty buffalo sauce pairs perfectly with golden, crispy potatoes for a satisfying bite. This easy, high-protein recipe is your new go-to for delicious, nutritious meals throughout the week!
tips: To make a low calorie ranch sauce (optional topping) mix together 100g low fat greek yoghurt & 1.5 Tsp of ranch seasoning.

When melting the cheese on the last step you can either bake it in the oven for approximately 5 minutes if using a skillet or if using a regular frying pan place it on a he stove on low - medium heat with a lid on top until the cheese melts.

The potatoes used in this recipe are called “Carisma” potatoes which contain 25% less carbs compared to standard potatoes but can be substituted for any other type such as russet potatoes, sweet potatoes etc.
steps: Start by grabbing your diced chicken breast, add the seasonings listed, then mix it together and set it aside.
Grab your diced potatoes, add a bit of olive oil with the same seasonings, mix well and air fry or oven bake until golden brown and crispy.
In a pan on medium heat, add light butter, followed by diced onion. Once it has some color, add the chicken and let it cook through.
Once the chicken is cooked, take it off the heat and add buffalo sauce with a bit of honey, then add the crispy potatoes and mix it all together.
Add a little bit of cheese on top, place it on the stove or back in the oven until the cheese is melted and golden brown.
Optionally, add more buffalo sauce and ranch, then divide into 4 equal servings and enjoy.

heading: Honey Garlic Chicken Tenders
description: Get ready to indulge in these mouthwatering High Protein Crispy Honey Garlic Chicken Tenders! Coated in a crunchy, golden-brown breadcrumb layer and drizzled with a sweet and savory honey garlic glaze, these tenders are not only delicious but also a fantastic source of protein. Perfect for a quick weeknight dinner or a satisfying snack, they’re sure to become your new favorite dish!
tips: Air fry: 180°C for 12-15 minutes
Oven bake: 190°C for 20-25 minutes
steps: To a plate add plain flour, the seasonings and spices and mix well.
Slice the chicken breast into three strips and coat them on all sides in the seasoned flour mixture.
On a separate plate add one beaten egg and a splash of pickle juice, mix then dunk the chicken breast in the egg mixture till fully coated.
On a third plate add corn flakes and press the chicken down firmly to get an even and crispy coating on all sides.
Give them a light spray with cooking oil and bake or air fry at 180°C for 12-15 minutes or oven bake at 190°C for 20-25 minutes.
Meanwhile, melt some butter in a pan on low heat and add soy sauce, three tablespoons of honey, garlic paste, and chili flakes, then bring to a bubble and simmer on low till the sauce thickens.
Lastly, place the crispy tenders in the sauce and roll them around till they're fully coated.

heading: Spicy Chicken Nuggets
description: Get ready to indulge in these crispy High Protein Spicy Nuggets, a guilt-free twist on your favorite fast-food treat! Packed with double the protein thanks to lean chicken and a zesty blend of spices, these nuggets are baked to perfection for a healthier crunch. Perfect for a quick snack or a fun meal, you'll love every spicy bite!
tips: Chill your chicken mince for 30 minutes after seasoning because cold proteins hold their shape better during coating, preventing the mixture from becoming sticky and hard to handle.
Blend your crisp coating to a specific texture - aim for 70% fine crumbs with 30% slightly larger pieces to create maximum surface area for oil adhesion while maintaining crunch.
Add 1 tablespoon of water per 100ml egg whites to thin the coating mixture, as pure egg whites are too thick and create uneven coating that prevents proper crumb adhesion.
Use a wire rack during baking because it allows hot air to circulate underneath, preventing soggy bottoms and ensuring even browning at 180°C.
Apply cooking oil spray in 2-3 light coats rather than one heavy spray, as this creates better Maillard reaction coverage without making the coating soggy.
steps: Add the chicken mince to a bowl with 1 Tsp of each seasonings. Then throw your hands in and mix till all the flavour is well incorporated
To make the crispy exterior, In a blender cup add the Popworks crisps with another Tsp of each seasoning and blend into fine crumbs. Pour those out onto a plate.

In a new bowl add egg whites and sriracha and mix to combine, then shake out 15-20 nuggets form your chicken mince and dunk them one by one in the wet mixture followed by the dry mixture. Make sure they are full coated in the crumbs.
Place them onto a baking rack and give them a quick spray of cooking oil. Then bake or airfry them at 180°C for 15 minutes.
Serve with your favourite low cal sauce and enjoy!

heading: Taco Mac and Cheese
description: Dive into a delicious bowl of Taco Mac and Cheese, where cheesy goodness meets zesty taco flavors! Packed with protein-rich ground turkey and creamy cheddar, this dish not only satisfies your cravings but also makes for a perfect make-ahead meal that you can freeze for quick, nutritious lunches all week long. Get ready to indulge in a comforting fusion that’s as easy to prepare as it is delightful to eat!
tips: Blend the cottage cheese mixture for 60-90 seconds until completely smooth because cottage cheese curds will create grainy texture if not fully broken down by the blender blades.
Cook ground beef to internal temperature of 160°F and drain fat before adding taco seasoning to prevent the seasoning from clumping in rendered fat.
Reserve 1/4 cup pasta cooking water before draining because the starch content (2-3%) helps bind the cottage cheese sauce to the pasta and prevents separation.
Let the blended cheese sauce rest for 2-3 minutes after blending so air bubbles dissipate and the cottage cheese proteins fully hydrate for smoother texture.
Add the cheese sauce to pasta while both are still warm (140-160°F) because heat helps the cottage cheese proteins coat the pasta more effectively than cold mixing.
steps: Combine one and a half cups of cottage cheese, half a cup of fat-free cheddar, one packet of mac and cheese powder, and half a cup of fat-free milk in a blender and blend until completely smooth.
In a pan, add 24 ounces of lean ground beef, half a cup of diced onions, and one taco seasoning packet. Cook on medium heat for 10 minutes.
In a bowl, combine the cooked protein pasta with the cheese sauce and cooked ground beef, mixing until fully combined.
Top with a drizzle of light ranch and fresh chives.

heading: Buffalo Popcorn Chicken
description: Get ready for a flavor explosion with our Buffalo Popcorn Chicken! This delightful dish combines crispy, golden-brown chicken bites tossed in zesty buffalo sauce, delivering a protein-packed punch that's perfect for a quick meal or snack. Toss in some crunchy popcorn for an irresistible twist that will have everyone coming back for more!
steps: Cube the chicken breast into small pieces.
In a bowl, add flour, baking powder, and spices, and mix well.
Place everything in a ziplock bag and shake it up.
Air fry at 375 degrees for 12-16 minutes until crispy.
Toss the cooked chicken in buffalo sauce.
Optional: Serve with lite ranch

heading: Cajun Chicken Burritos
description: Spice up your meal prep with these zesty Cajun Chicken Burritos! Packed with juicy, seasoned chicken, black beans, and fresh veggies, these burritos are a delicious way to keep your protein high and calories low. Wrap them up for an effortless, flavorful lunch or dinner that will keep you coming back for more!
tips: Air fry chicken breast at exactly 375°F and check internal temperature at 15 minutes - chicken continues cooking from residual heat and will reach 165°F during a 3-minute rest.
Blend the cottage cheese sauce for 90-120 seconds until completely smooth because under-blended cottage cheese creates grainy texture that won't coat the chicken evenly.
Toast assembled burritos seam-side down first for 2-3 minutes to seal the tortilla with steam before flipping - this prevents filling from leaking out.
Let the chicken-sauce mixture rest for 5 minutes before assembling so the cottage cheese proteins bind with chicken juices, creating better adhesion and flavor penetration.
Use a 2:1 ratio of chicken to sauce mixture by weight to prevent soggy tortillas while ensuring adequate moisture for flavor distribution.
steps: Combine 1 cup of cottage cheese, ½ a cup of grated parmesan, 1 tablespoon of Cajun seasoning, 2 garlic cloves, 1 tablespoon of tomato paste, and ½ a cup of fat-free milk and blend it all together until completely smooth.
Season 24 oz of chicken breast with garlic salt and Cajun seasoning until fully coated and air fry at 375 for 15-20 minutes.
Add the diced chicken to a bowl, along with the sauce, 1 cup of fat-free mozzarella, cooked bell peppers and onions, fresh chives, and mix it all together until fully combined.
Assemble by adding ¼ of the filling to a low-calorie tortilla, folding everything into a burrito, and toasting on both sides until golden brown.

heading: Creamy Sesame Butter Chicken
description: Get ready to elevate your meal prep game with our mouthwatering Creamy Sesame Butter Chicken! This protein-packed dish combines tender chicken with a rich, nutty sesame sauce, making it an irresistible addition to your weekly lineup. Perfect for easy portioning, you'll love the way this flavorful recipe transforms your lunches into something truly special!
ingredients: 200ml skimmed milk or milk of choice
tips: Dice chicken breast to uniform 3/4-inch cubes to ensure even cooking and prevent smaller pieces from overcooking and becoming rubbery while larger pieces remain undercooked.
Cook chicken on high heat for only 6-8 minutes instead of 10 to reach 165°F internal temperature without drying out the protein, as chicken breast loses 30% of its moisture when overcooked.
Temper your cream cheese to room temperature before adding to prevent curdling when it hits the hot pan, as cold dairy proteins seize at temperatures above 180°F.
Toast sesame seeds in a dry pan for 2-3 minutes until golden before adding to release volatile oils and increase nutty flavor compounds by up to 300%.
Use a 1.5:1 water-to-rice ratio and let rice rest off heat for 10 minutes after cooking to allow starches to fully gelatinize and prevent mushy texture during meal prep storage.
steps: Dice the raw chicken breast.
Season with garlic powder, cumin, black pepper, and sesame oil.
Mix together and cook on high heat in a pan for 10 minutes.
Then on low heat, add light butter, garlic, sesame seeds, skimmed milk, low fat cream cheese, chopped spinach, and the cooked chicken.
Stir and let it simmer for 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, boil the uncooked rice.
Add equal portions of the chicken mixture and rice to your prep containers.

heading: Methi Malayi Soya
description: Dive into a delightful dish with my Methi Malayi Soya, a must-try for anyone looking to savor a delicious vegetarian meal while supporting their fat loss goals! Infused with fragrant fenugreek leaves and creamy yogurt, this recipe showcases protein-packed soy chunks that make every bite a satisfying experience. Whip it up in no time for a flavorful addition to your healthy eating journey!
tips: Soak soy chunks in 80°C water for exactly 8-10 minutes to achieve optimal protein extraction while preventing the spongy texture that occurs with longer soaking times.
Create a slurry with corn flour using a 1:3 ratio (flour to cold milk) before adding to prevent lumping and achieve smooth thickening without breaking the emulsion.
Bloom whole spices (moti elaichi, cinnamon, tej patta) in oil at 160-170°C for 30-45 seconds to release fat-soluble compounds that increase flavor intensity by 300%.
Soak cashews in warm water (60°C) for 15 minutes before blending to reduce grinding time by 40% and create a silkier gravy texture.
Add milk in two stages - 70ml during blending and 30ml at the end - to prevent curdling from acid in tomatoes while maintaining creamy consistency.
steps: After cutting the onions, add them to a bowl with water.
Add moti elaichi, cinnamon, and tej patta, then place on the gas stove.
Add salt and 50 grams of soy chunks and boil them in more water.
Once boiled, remove impurities and add 20 grams of cashews and 100 ml of milk.
Add coriander leaves and blend to make a thick creamy gravy.
In a non-stick pan, add the gravy and mix with a little corn flour and remaining milk.
Finally, add the boiled soy chunks and mix well.

heading: Egg Sandwich
description: Whip up a deliciously nutritious egg sandwich that’s not only packed with protein but also super simple to prepare! Featuring fluffy scrambled eggs, creamy avocado, and a sprinkle of fresh herbs, this tasty treat is your go-to for a satisfying and wholesome meal any time of day. Dive into this scrumptious delight and fuel your body right!
tips: Mix egg yolks with butter at room temperature (68-72°F) to create proper emulsification - cold ingredients will result in a grainy, separated sauce instead of smooth creaminess.
Use full-fat dahi (Greek yogurt) with 10-15% fat content because the fat molecules help stabilize the sauce and prevent it from becoming watery when mixed with lemon juice.
Add lemon juice last and limit to 1-2 teaspoons maximum, as the acid will begin denaturing the proteins in the yogurt and egg yolks, potentially causing curdling after 10-15 minutes.
Toast bread to an internal temperature of 180-200°F to create a moisture barrier that prevents the yogurt-based sauce from making the bread soggy within 5 minutes of assembly.
Chop egg whites into 1/4-inch pieces rather than mashing them, as larger pieces retain their protein structure better and provide textural contrast against the creamy yolk sauce.
steps: In a bowl, add 6 egg yolks and half tablespoon butter. Mix well and crumble the yolk.
Add 1 katori dahi, salt, chaat masala, and black pepper. Mix everything well.
Add chopped coriander and a squeeze of lemon. Mix again to prepare the sauce.
Take some sauce out for the base of the sandwich.
Add chopped egg whites to the remaining mixture.
Toast the bread of your choice.
Assemble the sandwich: spread sauce on the base, add lettuce, more sauce, and the egg white mix. Top with sliced onions.
Enjoy your healthy egg sandwich!

heading: Honey Buffalo Chicken Tenders
description: Satisfy your cravings with these crispy, guilt-free honey buffalo chicken tenders! Lightly breaded and baked to perfection, these tenders are tossed in a sweet and spicy sauce made from tangy hot sauce and a drizzle of honey. Perfect for a fun dinner or game day snack, you’ll love indulging without the extra calories!
tips: Bake at 425°F for 18-20 minutes to achieve crispy cornflake coating without oil absorption - high heat creates steam that crisps from inside out.
Use a 2:1 ratio of buffalo sauce to honey in your glaze and reduce for 3-4 minutes until it coats the back of a spoon for proper adhesion to chicken.
Let chicken strips rest 15 minutes after seasoning to allow salt to penetrate and draw out surface moisture for better cornflake adhesion.
Crush cornflakes to pea-sized pieces rather than powder - larger pieces create air pockets that crisp better and provide textural contrast.
Add 1 tablespoon buffalo sauce per 2 eggs in your wash to create flavor bridges between coating and chicken while maintaining binding properties.
steps: Start by grabbing two chicken breasts, cut each one into five strips, add the seasonings listed on screen, mix it and set it to the side.
Then crack two eggs into a bowl and mix it together with a little bit of buffalo sauce.
On a separate plate add cornflakes with cayenne pepper and paprika then crush it into smaller pieces.
Dip each piece of chicken in the egg and coat it in the crushed cornflakes. Make sure to press down on it so it sticks.
Spray them with some light cooking oil spray then bake them in the oven or air fryer.
While that's cooking, to a pan on low heat add buffalo sauce and honey, mix it together until it's nice and thick.
Coat each piece of chicken in the sauce to finish it off and enjoy!

heading: Greek Style Chicken Salad
description: Dive into a vibrant bowl of Greek-inspired chicken salad that's bursting with flavor and perfect for low-carb enthusiasts! This zesty dish combines tender chicken with a creamy yogurt dressing, a kick of chili, and a sprinkle of fresh herbs, all whipped up in just 20 minutes. It's a protein-packed delight that’s as satisfying as it is speedy—your taste buds will thank you!
ingredients: salt, pepper, paprika, garlic powder and oregano
tips: Pound chicken breasts to 3/4-inch uniform thickness before seasoning because it ensures even cooking and prevents the thinner edges from overcooking while the thick center reaches 165°F.
Let seasoned chicken rest for 15-20 minutes at room temperature before cooking so salt penetrates the meat through osmosis, improving flavor and moisture retention.
Use full-fat Greek yogurt (10% fat minimum) for the dressing because the fat content prevents the proteins from curdling when acidic lemon juice is added.
Salt cucumber slices and let drain for 10 minutes before adding to salad because this removes excess water that would otherwise dilute the dressing and make the salad soggy.

Cook chicken to exactly 165°F internal temperature using a probe thermometer because at this point, salmonella is instantly killed while proteins haven't yet contracted enough to expel maximum moisture.
steps: Rub some olive oil under chicken breast, then season and pat down the following spices on both sides.
Cook on a medium heat pan, six to eight minutes on each side.
For the dressing, mix Greek yogurt, hot sauce, garlic, lemon, salt, pepper, dill, and a touch of water.
Assemble the salad with lettuce, tomatoes, cucumbers, onions, olives, and feta.
Add chicken and dressing, toss it up, and enjoy.

heading: Tuscan Butter Chicken
description: Dive into a bowl of pure deliciousness with our Spicy Tuscan Butter Chicken! This high-protein delight features tender chicken bathed in a luscious, creamy sauce infused with sun-dried tomatoes and fresh spinach. Perfectly seasoned and irresistibly rich, this dish will have your taste buds dancing!
tips: Pound chicken breast to 3/4-inch thickness before seasoning to ensure even cooking and prevent the outside from overcooking while the center reaches 165°F.
Add cream cheese in small cubes while whisking constantly to prevent breaking - the proteins in low-fat cream cheese are more prone to curdling at temperatures above 180°F.
Toast tomato paste in the pan for 60-90 seconds before adding liquids to develop deeper umami flavors through the Maillard reaction and eliminate raw metallic taste.
Use a 2:1 ratio of liquid to rice and let it rest off heat for 10 minutes after cooking so residual steam finishes the cooking process without making it mushy.
Add spinach in the final 2-3 minutes of simmering since it contains 90% water and will release liquid that can thin your sauce if cooked too long.
steps: Season the diced chicken breast with garlic powder, oregano, cumin, and olive oil. Mix together and cook in a pan on high heat for 10-15 minutes or in the oven/air fryer for 15 minutes at 220 °C.
On low heat, add light butter, garlic paste, chopped onion, tomato paste, skimmed milk, low fat cream cheese, spinach, and the cooked chicken. Stir and let it simmer until rich and creamy.
Meanwhile, boil the uncooked rice until cooked.

heading: Honey BBQ Chicken Stir Fry
description: Savor the mouthwatering blend of flavors in our Sticky Honey BBQ Chicken Stir Fry, a high-protein delight that's ideal for your meal prep game! With tender chicken, vibrant bell peppers, and a luscious honey BBQ glaze, this dish is not only easy to whip up but also packed with flavor. Get ready to enjoy a satisfying and nutritious meal that will have you coming back for seconds!
tips: Cut chicken breast into uniform 1-inch cubes to ensure even cooking and prevent smaller pieces from drying out while larger pieces remain undercooked.
Cook chicken to an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C) but remove from heat at 160°F as carryover cooking will bring it to safe temperature while maintaining juiciness.
Add honey during the last 2-3 minutes of cooking because its sugars will burn at temperatures above 320°F, creating bitter compounds instead of caramelization.
Use a rice-to-water ratio of 1:1.5 for firmer grains that won't become mushy when reheated, as overcooked rice loses 30% of its texture integrity after storage.
Add bell peppers 2 minutes before spinach since peppers need 3-4 minutes to soften while spinach wilts in under 60 seconds and releases excess moisture if overcooked.
steps: Boil 200g uncooked rice (roughly 630-650g of cooked rice).
Dice 600g raw chicken breast into cubes and season with 1 tablespoon garlic powder, 1 tablespoon oregano, 1 tablespoon paprika, and lemon juice.
Mix together and cook in a pan with 1 tablespoon of olive oil for 10-15 minutes on high heat (or oven/air fry for 15 minutes at 220 °C).
While cooking, add 20g of BBQ sauce and 20g of honey.
Chop up and add 100g of bell pepper and spinach.
Let it simmer until sticky and thick.
Divide into containers, roughly 125g of boiled rice per container, and enjoy.

heading: Chicken Fried Rice
description: Get ready to savor a bowl of deliciousness with our protein-packed chicken fried rice! This dish combines succulent chicken, vibrant veggies, and perfectly cooked rice, all stir-fried with a hint of soy sauce for an irresistible flavor boost. It's a fantastic meal prep choice that’s both satisfying and light on calories—ideal for keeping your weeknight dinners exciting and healthy!
tips: Use day-old rice that's been refrigerated because the starch molecules retrograde and firm up, preventing mushy texture and creating the ideal 15-20% moisture content for proper frying.
Heat your wok or pan to 400-450°F before adding ingredients because this temperature creates the Maillard reaction instantly and prevents rice from steaming in its own moisture.

Coat chicken cubes with cornstarch at a 1:8 ratio (1 tablespoon per 8 oz chicken) to create a protective barrier that locks in moisture while developing a crispy exterior through starch gelatinization.
Push ingredients to sides of the pan when adding eggs because this creates a 300°F+ zone in the center where eggs can set properly in 30-45 seconds without overcooking other ingredients.
Add sesame oil at the very end because its smoke point is only 350°F and prolonged high heat destroys its delicate nutty compounds, reducing flavor intensity by up to 60%.
steps: Take out 24 ounces of chicken breast, dice it into cubes, add the chicken to a bowl, and season with garlic powder, smoked paprika, onion powder, tablespoon of cornstarch, and 2 tablespoons of soy sauce, then mix it all together.
Cook the chicken on high heat until crispy, then add frozen vegetables to the pan and cook for 5 minutes, pushing them to the side.
Pour in 3 beaten eggs, slowly scramble them and mix in with the vegetables.
Add 20 ounces of day-old cooked rice, 2 tablespoons of soy sauce, and 1 tablespoon of sesame oil, then mix it all together and add in the chicken.
Top with sesame seeds and fresh green onions.

heading: Buffalo Ranch Chicken Nuggets
description: Craving a tasty snack that packs a protein punch? These Buffalo Ranch Chicken Nuggets are your new go-to! With zesty buffalo sauce, creamy ranch seasoning, and a crispy coating, you’ll enjoy every bite while hitting your protein goals—151g in one delicious serving!
ingredients: 1 tsp salt, pepper, garlic powder, & onion powder
tips: Chill your formed nuggets for 15-20 minutes before breading because cold protein holds its shape better during the dipping process and prevents the coating from becoming soggy.
Mix ground chicken just until combined (about 30-45 seconds) to avoid developing tough gluten strands that create dense, rubbery nuggets.
Add 1-2 tablespoons of mayonnaise to your ground chicken mixture because the fat creates moisture barriers and emulsifies for 25% juicier nuggets.
Spray oil in 2-3 light coats during cooking rather than one heavy coat because multiple thin layers create better Maillard browning at 350°F without burning the protein chip coating.
Let nuggets rest 3-4 minutes after the final buffalo sauce application so the sauce sets and doesn't drip off, maintaining coating adhesion.
steps: Add ground chicken, breadcrumbs, ranch seasoning, salt, pepper, garlic powder, and onion powder to a bowl and combine with hands.
In a second bowl, add protein chips, and crunch with hands.
In a third bowl, add two egg whites.
Form chicken into nuggets and dip in egg whites, then protein chips and set on tray of air fryer.
Spray with olive oil and air fry at 350°F (177°C) for 15 mins or until internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).
Brush with buffalo hot sauce and put back in air fryer for 2 mins.

heading: Crispy Chicken Sandwich
description: Sink your teeth into this Garlic-Infused Crispy Chicken Sandwich, a guilt-free delight that boasts nearly twice the protein of your favorite fast-food option! With a perfectly seasoned, crunchy coating and a zesty garlic marinade that elevates every bite, this sandwich is sure to crush those cravings while keeping you fueled and satisfied. Get ready to savor a homemade twist on a classic favorite!
tips: Marinate chicken thighs in Greek yogurt for 2-4 hours minimum because the lactic acid breaks down proteins while enzymes tenderize, increasing moisture retention by 15-20%.
Pat chicken completely dry after marinating and before breading because excess moisture creates steam that prevents Maillard reaction, reducing crispiness by up to 60%.
Bake at 425°F and flip halfway through cooking to ensure even browning on both sides, as Panko needs 400°F+ to achieve maximum crispiness through moisture evaporation.
Use a 3:1 ratio of Panko to seasoning mix because too much seasoning creates a dense coating that inhibits the light, airy texture Panko provides.
Rest the breaded chicken for 10-15 minutes before cooking so the coating adheres properly and won't fall off during the cooking process.
steps: Add chicken thighs to a bowl with Greek yoghurt, garlic paste, onion powder, oregano, a dash of pepper, and lemon juice. Mix until well coated.
In a separate bowl, combine Panko breadcrumbs with paprika, onion powder, salt, and pepper.
Dunk the chicken thighs in the breadcrumb mixture, pressing down firmly until fully covered.
Spray the coated chicken with oil and bake or air fry until cooked through.
On a toasted brioche bun, add garlic mayo, lettuce, crispy chicken, a slice of light cheese, and the top bun.

heading: Double McChicken
description: Indulge in a guilt-free twist on the beloved McChicken with our Easy & Healthy Double McChicken! This mouthwatering burger features lean ground chicken and a zesty yogurt-based sauce, all sandwiched between whole grain buns for a satisfying crunch. Enjoy a delicious, protein-packed meal that won't weigh you down—perfect for any day of the week!
tips: Chill your formed chicken patties in the freezer for 15-20 minutes before breading - this firms up the proteins and prevents the patties from falling apart during coating and cooking.
Keep your hands wet when forming chicken mince patties because moisture prevents the meat from sticking to your palms and creates smoother, more uniform patties.
Air fry chicken patties at 375°F for 12-15 minutes, flipping halfway through, to achieve golden browning through the Maillard reaction while maintaining 165°F internal temperature.
Mix breadcrumbs with 1 teaspoon of oil per cup before seasoning to help them adhere better and brown more evenly during cooking.
Toast your brioche buns cut-side down in a dry pan for 60-90 seconds to create a moisture barrier that prevents the sauce from making the bread soggy.
steps: In a bowl add chicken mince followed by a tiny bit of pepper then add the seasonings listed.
Make sure your hands are wet, mix it together and form 4 even patties, then place them on baking paper and in the freezer to let them firm up.
In a bowl add light mayo, mustard, garlic powder, mix it to create the sauce.
On a plate add breadcrumbs with paprika and the same seasonings used before, then coat each piece of chicken evenly.
Spray them with light cooking oil spray then air fry or bake them.
Grab a toasted brioche bun, add half of the sauce with 2 pieces of chicken and lettuce, repeat for the other McChicken.

heading: Chicken Egg Fried Rice
description: Elevate your meal prep game with this scrumptious chicken fried rice that packs a whopping 50g of protein and just 487 calories per serving! Featuring tender chicken, vibrant veggies, and a drizzle of soy sauce, this dish brings restaurant-quality flavors right to your kitchen. Get ready to enjoy a satisfying, wholesome meal that’s as easy to make as it is delicious!
tips: Use day-old rice that's been refrigerated because the starches retrograde and firm up, reducing moisture content by 15-20% to prevent mushy fried rice.
Heat your wok or pan to 400-450°F before adding oil so proteins sear immediately and create the 'wok hei' smoky flavor through Maillard reactions.
Cook chicken in small batches (150g maximum) to maintain pan temperature and achieve proper browning instead of steaming.
Add frozen vegetables directly to the hot pan without thawing because the ice crystals create steam that cooks them in exactly 60-90 seconds while maintaining crunch.
Create a well in your pan for scrambling eggs because the direct metal contact at 300°F+ creates fluffy curds in 30-45 seconds before they overcook.
steps: Dice up 600 grams of chicken breast and add some soy sauce, 10ml of sesame oil, garlic powder, and black pepper. Mix until well-coated.
In a pan, add light cooking spray and cook the chicken in batches until crispy.
Add frozen mixed vegetables and cook for a minute or two, then push to the side and pour in four beaten eggs.
Turn the heat down and stir the eggs gently every minute until fluffy.
Mix the eggs with the vegetables and add 600 grams of day-old cooked rice, more soy sauce, and 30 grams of oyster sauce.
Add the white parts of the spring onions, mix thoroughly, and divide into four servings.

heading: Chipotle Garlic Chicken Pasta
description: Whip up a delicious batch of Creamy Chipotle Garlic Chicken Pasta in just 30 minutes! This mouthwatering dish features tender chicken, zesty chipotle sauce, and a luscious creaminess that’s bursting with flavor—all while keeping each serving under 500 calories. Perfect for meal prep, it’s a protein-packed delight that will have your taste buds dancing!
ingredients: 1/3 cup light / fat free cream cheese
tips: Heat your pan to 325-350°F before adding chicken to achieve proper Maillard reaction without overcooking the interior, ensuring juicy results in the 6-8 minute timeframe.
Reserve exactly 1 cup of starchy pasta water before draining because the starches act as natural emulsifiers, helping bind the cream cheese sauce without breaking.
Blend the sauce while hot (above 140°F) so the cream cheese melts completely and creates a silky emulsion rather than lumpy texture.
Use chipotle peppers in adobo rather than dried chipotles because the adobo sauce contains vinegar and sugar that balance the heat while adding depth.
Toss pasta with sauce off heat for 30-60 seconds to allow starches to fully hydrate and thicken the sauce through mechanical action.
steps: Add olive oil to chicken breast and season with salt, pepper, paprika, chili powder, and garlic powder on both sides.
Cook chicken in a medium heat pan for six to eight minutes on each side, then set aside.
In the same pan, add canned tomatoes, garlic, and chopped onions, and cook until soft.
Transfer to a blender with chipotle peppers, light cream cheese, low fat cheese, a pinch of salt, and pasta water. Blend until smooth.
Add the sauce back to the pan and mix in the cooked pasta.
Serve with optional chopped cilantro on top.

heading: Lemon Pepper Chicken
description: Dive into this zesty Creamy Lemon Pepper Chicken, your new go-to for meal prep! Featuring tender chicken breasts simmered in a luscious lemon-infused sauce and a kick of black pepper, this dish is not just packed with protein but bursting with flavor. Whip it up in no time and savor delicious, wholesome meals all week long!
tips: Pound chicken to 3/4-inch thickness before chopping to ensure even cooking and prevent overcooking the smaller pieces while larger ones remain undercooked.
Cook chicken to exactly 165°F internal temperature, then remove from heat immediately as carryover cooking will add another 5-10°F and prevent protein from becoming stringy.
Temper your cream cheese to room temperature and whisk it with 2-3 tablespoons of the hot milk before adding to the pan to prevent curdling from temperature shock.
Add spinach in the final 30-45 seconds of cooking to preserve its bright color and prevent nutrient loss from overcooking water-soluble vitamins.
Cool the creamy sauce to below 140°F before portioning into containers to prevent condensation buildup that dilutes flavors and creates food safety risks during storage.
steps: Chop your chicken, add garlic, black pepper, lemon juice, and olive oil. Mix together and cook for 10-15 minutes on high heat.

When on low heat, add light butter, garlic paste, chopped onion, lemon juice, black pepper, milk, low-fat cream cheese, spinach, and the cooked chicken. Stir together and let it simmer until rich and creamy.
Meanwhile, cook your fluffy rice and add equal portions to your meal prep containers.

heading: Garlic Pepper Chicken
description: Savor the flavors of our creamy garlic pepper chicken, a protein-packed delight that’s ideal for your weekly meal prep! This dish features succulent chicken breasts infused with zesty garlic and a hint of black pepper, all simmered to perfection in a luscious sauce. Whip up a batch and enjoy tasty, satisfying portions that keep you energized throughout the week!
tips: Pound chicken breasts to 3/4-inch uniform thickness before seasoning to ensure even cooking and prevent overcooking the thinner edges while the thick center reaches 165°F.
Cook chicken at medium-high heat (325-350°F pan temperature) instead of high heat to prevent moisture loss - high heat causes proteins to contract rapidly and expel up to 25% more juices.
Add cream when pan temperature drops to 180-200°F to prevent curdling, as dairy proteins denature and separate above 212°F when combined with acid from lemon juice.
Use a 1:2.5 rice-to-water ratio (200g rice to 500ml water) for meal prep rice that maintains texture after refrigeration and reheating without becoming mushy.
Cool chicken completely to 40°F before portioning into containers to prevent condensation buildup that dilutes the sauce and creates food safety risks in the temperature danger zone.
steps: Mix together chicken breast, garlic powder, salt, oregano, and cumin. Cook for 10 minutes on high heat until golden.
On low heat, add light butter, garlic, chopped onion, chopped mushroom, lemon juice, low fat cream, and cooked chicken. Stir together and let it simmer until rich and creamy.
Meanwhile, boil 200g of uncooked rice (approximately 630g cooked rice) and add leftover sauce from the pan to the chicken.

heading: Cheese Pizza
description: Indulge in a guilt-free slice of our Easy High Protein Cheese Pizza! Packed with lean protein and topped with melty mozzarella, this delightful dish is not only a treat for your taste buds but also a smart choice for your fitness goals. Whip it up with a whole wheat crust for an extra nutrient boost and enjoy a satisfying meal that keeps you on track!
ingredients: Salt, Italian Seasoning Blend & Garlic Powder
tips: Bake the protein pizza base at 425°F for 12-15 minutes before adding toppings to prevent soggy crust, as the high moisture content from yogurt and egg whites needs proper evaporation.
Use a 2:1 ratio of self-raising flour to coconut flour because coconut flour absorbs 4x more liquid than wheat flour, preventing an overly dense or crumbly texture.
Spread the dough to exactly 1/4 inch thickness using an offset spatula - thinner creates brittle spots while thicker won't cook through properly with the high protein density.
Preheat your baking stone or inverted sheet pan to 450°F for 30 minutes before baking to create immediate bottom heat that compensates for the dough's low gluten structure.
Pat mozzarella dry with paper towels and use 25% less cheese than regular pizza since the protein base retains more moisture and excess cheese will create steam pockets.
steps: In a bowl add self-raising flour, coconut flour, nutritional yeast, salt, Italian seasoning, garlic powder then mix it together.
Now add egg whites, low-fat yogurt then mix it until it's a thick and wet paste.
Use a spoon to spread it out on baking paper as big as you can and bake it in the oven until it slightly crisps up.
Add pizza sauce, minced garlic, parmesan cheese and low-fat mozzarella then bake it again until the cheese is golden brown.
Cut it up and enjoy!

heading: Crispy Sticky Orange Chicken
description: Elevate your meal prep game with this scrumptious high-protein crispy sticky orange chicken! Bursting with zesty citrus flavor and featuring tender chicken pieces coated in a crunchy glaze, this dish is not only satisfying but also packed with protein. Get ready to indulge in a healthy feast that’s perfect for your weekly lineup!
tips: Coat chicken pieces in cornflour at a 3:1 ratio (chicken to cornflour by weight) and let rest 10 minutes before cooking - the starch absorbs surface moisture and creates maximum crispy texture through the Maillard reaction.

Cook chicken at medium-high heat (325-350°F oil temperature) for the first 8 minutes, then reduce to medium for final 2-7 minutes to ensure internal temperature reaches 165°F while maintaining crispy exterior.
Mix cornflour slurry at exactly 1:2 ratio (cornflour to cold water) and add gradually while stirring - this prevents lumping and creates glossy sauce that coats at 180-185°F without breaking.
Use low-sodium soy sauce and reduce it by 25% during sauce simmering to concentrate umami compounds while preventing over-salting during meal prep storage.
Cool completely to room temperature (70°F) before portioning into containers - hot food creates condensation that makes crispy coating soggy within 2-3 hours.
steps: Chop your chicken. Add garlic powder, light soy sauce, black pepper, and cornflour to make it crispy. Mix together and cook with olive oil for 10-15 minutes until golden brown and crunchy.

On medium heat, add garlic, light soy sauce, low sugar orange juice, honey, sriracha, cornflour mixed with water, and the cooked chicken. Stir and let it simmer until thick. Finish with spring onion and portion into meal prep containers.

heading: Chicken Alfredo Pizza
description: Dive into our High Protein Chicken Alfredo Pizza, where creamy Alfredo sauce meets tender, grilled chicken for a mouthwatering experience! Packed with over 100 grams of protein, this delicious dish features a homemade whole wheat crust and a sprinkle of fresh spinach for an extra nutrient boost. Satisfy your cravings without the guilt—this pizza is a game-changer!
tips: Knead your yogurt-flour dough for exactly 3-4 minutes until smooth because over-kneading develops too much gluten, making the crust tough instead of tender.
Pre-bake your pizza crust at 425°F for 8-10 minutes until just set but not browned, preventing a soggy bottom when you add the wet Alfredo sauce.
Blend cottage cheese for 60-90 seconds until completely smooth because any remaining curds will create a grainy Alfredo sauce texture.
Heat your cottage cheese Alfredo sauce to exactly 165°F while stirring constantly because higher temps cause proteins to seize and separate.
Pat cooked chicken completely dry with paper towels before adding to pizza because excess moisture creates steam that makes cheese rubbery and prevents proper browning.
steps: Mix together flour, yogurt, Italian seasoning, salt, garlic powder and baking powder until you get a crumbly texture, then knead it to form a dough ball.
On a lightly floured surface, roll the dough out slowly and gently, then roll the edges over to form a crust. Bake it in the oven to let it slightly crisp up.
In a blender, add low-fat cottage cheese, fat-free milk, grated parmesan and Italian seasoning, then blend until smooth.
In a pan on low heat, add diced onion, then add the protein Alfredo sauce and mix until it thickens.
Spread the sauce on the base, then add low-fat cheese, cooked chicken, more onion, Italian seasoning and bake again.

heading: Chicken Nuggets
description: Get ready to indulge guilt-free with our zesty Spicy Chicken Nuggets! Packed with a whopping 40 grams of protein yet only 257 calories, these crispy bites are made with lean chicken breast and a fiery blend of spices that will tantalize your taste buds. Perfect for a quick snack or a protein-rich meal, these nuggets are sure to be a hit at your next gathering!
tips: Chill your seasoned chicken mixture for 30 minutes before forming nuggets - this firms up the proteins and reduces moisture migration, preventing coating separation during cooking.
Air fry at exactly 375°F because this temperature creates the Maillard reaction for browning while keeping internal temp at 165°F without overcooking the ground chicken's delicate texture.
Use hot sauce at 2-3% of your chicken weight as a binder - the vinegar denatures surface proteins creating better adhesion than eggs while adding moisture and flavor.
Pulse your protein chips to create mixed particle sizes (50% fine crumbs, 50% small chunks) - this creates better texture contrast and prevents uniform coating that can become soggy.
Flip nuggets at the 5-minute mark to ensure even browning, as air circulation creates hot spots that can cause 30% uneven cooking on the bottom side.
steps: Start with ground chicken and season with cayenne, paprika, garlic powder, and salt.
Add hot sauce to act as a binder and mix well.
Crush or blend the protein chips for a smoother coating.
Gently coat the chicken with the crushed chips.
Air fry at 375 degrees for 8-10 minutes.

heading: Garlic Chicken Fried Rice
description: Savor the irresistible flavors of our High-Protein Garlic Chicken Fried Rice, a scrumptious dish that’s both satisfying and simple to prepare! Packed with tender chicken, fragrant garlic, and vibrant veggies, this wholesome meal is a fantastic way to fuel your body while keeping your taste buds dancing. Dive into this guilt-free delight that’s perfect for any time you crave a healthy yet hearty bite!
tips: Cook chicken to exactly 165°F internal temperature, then rest 3-4 minutes before adding back to prevent overcooking and maintain 25% higher protein retention.
Use day-old rice with 12-15% moisture content because fresh rice's 20%+ moisture creates mushy texture instead of distinct grains when stir-fried.
Heat your wok or pan to 400-450°F before adding oil so ingredients sear immediately rather than steam, creating the signature 'wok hei' flavor.
Cook garlic for exactly 30 seconds at medium-high heat because allicin compounds peak at this timing—longer cooking destroys flavor, shorter doesn't develop it.
Add soy sauce to the pan sides first, not directly on rice, so it caramelizes at 280°F+ and creates deeper umami before coating the grains.
steps: Mix chicken breast, ginger garlic paste, chilli powder, and salt well, then cook it for 5 minutes.
In the same pan, heat oil and cook diced garlic until fragrant for 30 seconds, then add onion and cook until translucent.
Add bell peppers, ketchup, and soya sauce, mix well, and cook for 3 minutes.
Sprinkle pepper, add cooked rice, squeeze lime, and mix well.
Add the cooked chicken back in, mix again, and garnish with spring onions.

heading: Garlic Chicken Chow Mein
description: Get ready to elevate your meal prep game with this mouthwatering Garlic Chicken Chow Mein! Packed with protein-rich chicken and perfectly stir-fried noodles, this dish combines savory garlic and fresh veggies for a quick, satisfying meal. Whip it up in no time and enjoy flavorful, nutritious lunches all week long!
tips: Velvet your chicken by marinating in 1 egg white, 1 tbsp cornstarch, and 1 tsp oil for 30 minutes before cooking - this creates a protective coating that keeps protein fibers from contracting and losing moisture.
Cook chicken to exactly 165°F internal temperature, then immediately remove from heat as carryover cooking will raise it 5-10°F more while preserving maximum protein structure.
Use day-old refrigerated noodles or fresh noodles tossed with 1 tsp oil because cold, slightly dehydrated noodles absorb sauce better and won't become mushy during the 5-minute simmer.
Heat your wok or pan to 400-450°F before adding ingredients - this high heat creates the 'wok hei' (breath of the wok) flavor through Maillard reactions that occur above 300°F.
Add aromatics like garlic in the final 30 seconds of the chicken cooking phase to prevent burning while still allowing volatile compounds to bloom and infuse the oil.
steps: Slice your chicken and add garlic powder, black powder, salt, soy sauce, and sesame oil. Mix together and cook for 10 minutes on high heat.

Then on medium heat, add garlic, red bell pepper, spring onions, soy sauce, your cooked noodles, and your cooked chicken. Stir together and let it simmer for 5 minutes. Finish with sesame seeds and portion into meal prep containers.

heading: Buffalo Chicken Loaded Fries
description: Get ready to indulge in a flavor explosion with our Buffalo Loaded Fries! This mouthwatering creation combines golden, crispy fries smothered in tender, spicy buffalo chicken and drizzled with melted cheese for the ultimate comfort food experience. Perfect for game day or a cozy night in, these loaded fries are sure to satisfy your cravings!
tips: Soak cut potatoes in cold water for 30 minutes before seasoning to remove excess starch, which prevents proper crisping and can make fries gummy.
Use 1-2 tablespoons of flour per pound of potatoes to create a light coating that absorbs moisture and promotes Maillard browning at 375°F.
Double-fry method in air fryer: first round at 350°F for 15 minutes, then increase to 400°F for final 5-8 minutes to achieve maximum crispiness through moisture evaporation.
Use a 2:1 ratio of buffalo sauce to ranch when mixing with chicken to prevent the coating from becoming too thin and sliding off the fries.
Broil the final cheese layer 4-6 inches from heat source for 2-3 minutes maximum to achieve golden browning without overcooking the bottom components.
steps: Chop potatoes into fry shaped pieces.
Put in bowl, oil and season (Cajun, salt, pepper) add flour.
Air fry on 375 for 5 minutes, sift around, and repeat until desired crunchiness.
Plate fries.
Add layer of cheese.
Add cooked, diced chicken to a bowl and add Buffalo sauce and ranch and mix.
Add chicken on top of fries.
Add a final layer of cheese and broil until cheese turns golden brown.
Enjoy!

heading: Pepperoni Pizza
description: Craving pizza without the guilt? Dive into this deliciously satisfying Low Calorie & High Protein Pepperoni Pizza that's a breeze to whip up at home! With a crispy cauliflower crust and lean turkey pepperoni, you can indulge your taste buds while keeping your health goals in check.
tips: Bake your protein pizza base at 425°F for 12-15 minutes before adding toppings to create a moisture barrier that prevents sogginess from wet toppings.
Use a 2:1 ratio of self-raising flour to coconut flour because coconut flour absorbs 4x more liquid than wheat flour, creating the right dough consistency without excess moisture.
Whip egg whites to soft peaks before folding into the flour mixture to incorporate air and increase base volume by 30-40% for a lighter texture.
Pre-bake pepperoni slices at 375°F for 5 minutes on paper towels to render excess fat and prevent oil pools that make low-fat cheese greasy.
Use Greek yogurt at exactly 2% fat content rather than 0% because some fat is needed for proper dough binding while maintaining protein density of 15-18g per serving.
steps: In a bowl add self-raising flour, coconut flour, nutritional yeast, Italian seasoning, garlic powder, then mix it together.
Then add egg whites and low-fat yogurt, mix it so that you have a thick paste.
Using a spoon, spread it out on baking paper into a large circle.
Once the base is above average size, bake it to let it slightly crisp up.
Add pizza sauce, low-fat cheese, and beef pepperoni.
Bake it again one more time.
Add a bit of honey if desired, cut it up and enjoy.

heading: Crispy Chicken Sandwich
description: Get ready to sink your teeth into this irresistible crispy chicken sandwich that's a breeze to whip up in your own kitchen! With a golden, crunchy coating and juicy chicken that's loaded with nearly 50 grams of protein, you'll love every satisfying bite. Toss in some fresh lettuce and a zesty sauce, and you've got a mouthwatering meal that’s sure to impress!
tips: Pound chicken to exactly 1/2 inch thickness using a meat mallet because uniform thickness ensures even cooking and prevents the thin edges from overcooking while thick parts remain underdone.
Let breaded chicken rest for 15-20 minutes in the refrigerator before air frying so the coating adheres better and moisture creates steam that helps cornflakes bond to the protein.
Crush cornflakes to pea-sized pieces rather than powder because larger fragments create more surface area for oil adhesion and maintain crunch at 375°F air fryer temperatures.
Air fry at 375°F for 12-14 minutes, flipping at the 7-minute mark when the bottom develops golden color, because this timing allows the coating to set before flipping prevents breading loss.
Toast brioche cut-side down for 60-90 seconds until golden because the Maillard reaction creates a moisture barrier that prevents the bun from becoming soggy from chicken juices and sauce.
steps: Grab one large chicken breast. Tenderize until flattened. Slice in half. Season with salt and garlic powder.
Build the breading stations: Station 1 is flour, salt, garlic, and pepper. Next, egg whites and hot sauce. For the crispy exterior, use crushed cornflakes.
Dunk your chicken in the flour, egg mixture, and press into the cornflakes. Press it down to make sure it's fully coated.
Hit it with a light oil spray. Toss in the air fryer and flip halfway.
Lightly toast some brioche buns. Top with the low-calorie sriracha sauce using the ingredients listed.
Finish with pickles and your crispy chicken.

heading: Crispy Chicken Sandwich
description: Sink your teeth into this mouthwatering crispy chicken sandwich that delivers all the flavor without the calories! Featuring juicy, seasoned chicken coated in a light, crunchy breading and served on a fluffy bun, this guilt-free delight is topped with fresh greens and zesty sauce for an irresistible finish. Perfect for a satisfying meal that keeps you feeling great!
tips: Pound chicken to exactly 1/2 inch thickness using a meat mallet because uniform thickness ensures even cooking and prevents dry edges while the center reaches 165°F.
Crush cornflakes to 1/4 inch pieces maximum - larger pieces fall off during cooking while smaller pieces burn before the chicken cooks through.
Let breaded chicken rest for 10-15 minutes before cooking so the coating adheres properly and moisture creates steam that helps the crust bond to the meat.
Spray oil in 3-4 light coats during cooking rather than one heavy application because multiple thin layers create better browning without making the coating soggy.
Toast brioche buns cut-side down for 2-3 minutes until golden because the caramelized surface creates a moisture barrier that prevents the sauce from making the bread soggy.
steps: Grab your chicken breast and beat your meat to slightly flatten it then season each side with the seasonings listed.
Crush cornflakes into smaller pieces and mix with a dash of the same seasonings.
On a separate plate, mix together an egg with hot sauce.
Dip each piece of chicken in flour, then in the egg, and then in the cornflakes.
Spray with light cooking oil and bake in the oven or air fryer.
While baking, mix yogurt or light mayo with honey, smoked paprika, and hot sauce.
Grab a toasted brioche bun, add some sauce, pickles, the chicken, and more sauce, then repeat for the second bun.

heading: McMuffins
description: Kickstart your mornings with these deliciously wholesome McMuffins that pack a protein punch without the guilt! With simple staples like egg whites and lean turkey sausage, these beauties are not only easy to whip up but perfect for meal prepping ahead of a busy week. Get ready to savor every bite while staying on track with your health goals!
ingredients: 24g Diced & Cooked Bacon or Turkey Bacon
tips: Bake your egg sheet at 325°F for exactly 12-15 minutes until center reaches 160°F internal temperature to prevent rubbery texture from overcooking while ensuring food safety.
Press beef patties to 1/4-inch thickness with a slight dimple in center because meat contracts during cooking and will puff up to create uneven cooking.
Let cream cheese sit at room temperature for 30-45 minutes before mixing to achieve proper emulsification without lumps that occur when cold fats resist incorporation.
Wrap assembled McMuffins in parchment paper before plastic wrap to prevent moisture condensation that makes English muffins soggy during storage.
Cook beef patties on a preheated surface at 400°F+ for proper Maillard reaction browning in just 2 minutes per side without overcooking the thin patties.
steps: Start by cracking 6 eggs into a bowl with some salt and pepper, then mix it all together and pour it into a greased baking dish. Throw in some chives and bake it in the oven for 5-10 minutes, then take it out and cut it into 6 even slices.
In a bowl add light cream cheese and soften it with a spoon, then add diced jalapenos, mix it together and set it aside.
Grab some extra lean beef mince and add the seasonings listed, mix it together and form 6 even patties. Flatten each patty and cook it on high heat for around 2 minutes on each side.

Grab 6 toasted English breakfast muffins, add some of the cream cheese, diced bacon or turkey bacon, cheddar cheese, egg, the patty, repeat the process for the other 5, wrap them up, store them in the fridge or freezer and reheat when they're ready to eat.

heading: Mexican Style Chicken & Rice
description: Get ready to spice up your meal prep with this zesty Mexican-inspired chicken and rice dish that's packed with protein! Featuring succulent grilled chicken marinated in vibrant lime and chili, paired with fluffy cilantro-lime rice, this recipe is a flavor fiesta that will keep your taste buds dancing all week long. Perfect for a quick and satisfying lunch or dinner, you'll love how easy it is to whip up a batch!
ingredients: 1 tsp of each seasoning used previously
tips: Toast your rice for 2-3 minutes in the pan before adding liquid to develop nutty flavors through the Maillard reaction and create a firmer texture that prevents mushy meal prep containers.
Use a 1.5:1 water-to-rice ratio instead of 2:1 for meal prep rice because it reduces excess moisture that causes sogginess during refrigerated storage over 3-5 days.
Cook chicken cubes to exactly 165°F internal temperature, then rest 3 minutes before mixing with rice to prevent overcooking from residual heat and maintain 25% higher protein retention.
Add lime juice after cooking rather than during marinading because citric acid denatures proteins too aggressively when exposed to heat, creating a tough, rubbery texture.
Cool completely to room temperature before portioning into containers to prevent condensation buildup that reduces shelf life from 5 days to 2-3 days.
steps: Slice your chicken into cubes. Add oregano, garlic powder, paprika, cumin, chili powder, salt, pepper, and squeeze of lime juice. Mix until well combined, then cook on medium heat for 8 minutes until golden brown.

Set the chicken aside, then add onions and bell peppers. Cook for 2 minutes before adding black beans, washed rice, and more seasoning. Let it toast, then add chopped tomatoes with water. Bring to a light bubble, then cover and simmer for 12 minutes until fully cooked.
Mix in the chicken with coriander, then distribute into 4 equal servings and enjoy.

heading: Crispy Orange Chicken
description: Craving that beloved orange chicken but want to keep your fitness goals in check? Dive into our High Protein Crispy Orange Chicken! This zesty dish swaps traditional breading for a protein-packed crunch and bursts with fresh orange juice and zesty ginger, making it a guilt-free takeout delight you can whip up right at home!
tips: Double-coat your chicken by dredging in cornflour, dipping in beaten egg, then coating again in cornflour mixed with 1 tsp baking powder per cup - this creates maximum surface area for crispiness through steam leavening.
Fry chicken at exactly 350°F and don't overcrowd the pan - maintain at least 2 inches between pieces so oil temperature doesn't drop below 325°F, which causes soggy coating.
Bloom your aromatics (garlic, ginger, chili) for exactly 30-45 seconds until fragrant but not brown - overcooked garlic becomes bitter and masks the orange flavor profile.
Mix your cornflour slurry at a 1:2 ratio (cornflour to cold water) and add it gradually while whisking - this prevents lumps and creates a glossy sauce that coats at exactly 185°F.
Add the fried chicken to sauce off the heat and toss for only 15-20 seconds - longer contact time makes the coating soggy as steam softens the cornflour crust.
steps: First off, get yourself four medium chicken breasts, dice them up, and add some color with the seasonings.
To get that crispy texture, add cornflour and mix until well-coated.
Cook the chicken in batches to get it extra crispy, then set it aside.
Make the sticky orange sauce by adding garlic, ginger, spring onions, and red chili in a pan and toast until fragrant.
Add soy sauce, fresh orange juice, cornflour mixed with water, rice wine vinegar, and orange zest.
Turn up the heat to get it bubbling, then simmer on low heat until thick.
Add the chicken back into the sauce and mix until coated.

heading: Chili Lime Chicken Burritos
description: Get ready to spice up your meal prep with these zesty Chili Lime Chicken Burritos! In just 20 minutes, you'll enjoy tender chicken marinated in a tangy chili-lime blend, wrapped in warm tortillas with fresh toppings like crisp lettuce and creamy avocado. Packed with protein and bursting with flavor, these burritos are a deliciously healthy option for any day of the week!
ingredients: 1/4 tsp chili powder and garlic powder
1/2 tsp salt, pepper, chili powder, cumin
tips: Cook chicken breast to exactly 165°F internal temperature, then immediately rest in residual pan heat for 3-4 minutes to allow carryover cooking to reach 170°F while retaining maximum moisture.
Add lime juice only in the final 2-3 minutes of cooking chicken because acid denatures proteins too quickly, creating a tough, stringy texture if cooked too long.
Toast your tortillas for 30-45 seconds per side in a dry pan until they develop light brown spots - this creates a moisture barrier that prevents sogginess and adds structural integrity.
Use a 3:1 ratio of mayo to lime juice in your sauce because higher acid ratios break the emulsion, causing separation and watery texture.
Wrap assembled burritos in foil and rest seam-side down for 5 minutes before cutting - this allows the tortilla's gluten to relax and seal, preventing unwrapping.
steps: Cook the chicken breast in olive oil with salt, pepper, chili powder, cumin, paprika, garlic powder, and lime juice until fully cooked.
Mix the light mayo (or Greek yogurt) with hot sauce, chili powder, garlic powder, and lime juice to create the chili lime sauce.
Assemble the burritos by placing the cooked chicken, chili lime sauce, cheese, lettuce, tomato, and red onion in the tortillas. Roll them up and enjoy!

heading: Chicken Smash Burger
description: Get ready to savor a deliciously juicy Chicken Smash Burger that packs a protein punch with 35 grams per serving—all for just 395 calories! This recipe features lean ground chicken seasoned to perfection and smashed onto a hot griddle for that irresistible crispy edge. Topped with fresh veggies and your favorite sauce, this burger is both a guilt-free treat and a flavor explosion you won’t want to miss!
tips: Chill your ground chicken mixture for 15-20 minutes before forming patties - cold protein holds together better during the aggressive smashing process and prevents the meat from sticking to your spatula.
Heat your pan to 400-425°F before adding patties because chicken needs higher heat than beef to develop proper Maillard browning while cooking through safely to 165°F internal temperature.
Smash patties within the first 30 seconds of hitting the pan when proteins are still pliable - after this window, muscle fibers set and won't spread properly, reducing surface area for browning.
Add 1-2 tablespoons of mayonnaise to your ground chicken mixture to increase fat content from 7% to 15%, preventing the lean meat from drying out during the high-heat smashing process.
Let pickle juice in your sauce sit for 10 minutes before serving - the acidity needs time to mellow and integrate with the mayo's emulsion, preventing a harsh vinegar bite.
steps: Combine 1 pound of ground chicken breast, ½ cup of diced onion, garlic salt, smoked paprika, and onion powder, mix it all together and divide into 4 equal portions.
Add the patties to a hot pan, smash them down, cook on both sides until golden brown, and melt a slice of cheese on top.
For the sauce, combine 4 tablespoons of light mayo, 2 tablespoons of ketchup, 1 tablespoon of mustard, diced pickle, and a splash of pickle juice, and mix until smooth.
Assemble by spreading 1 ounce of guacamole on the bottom bun, adding the chicken patty, 1 tablespoon of sauce, and the top bun with more guacamole.

heading: Raising Canes
description: Indulge in a guilt-free feast with this scrumptious Healthy High Protein Raising Canes recipe! Featuring crispy, oven-baked chicken tenders coated in a zesty blend of spices and paired with a creamy, homemade dipping sauce, this dish delivers all the flavor without the extra calories. Get ready to satisfy your cravings while fueling your body with protein-packed goodness!
tips: Crush cornflakes to 1/4-inch pieces maximum because larger pieces burn before chicken reaches 165°F internal temperature, while smaller pieces create better adhesion through increased surface area.
Pound chicken strips to uniform 1/2-inch thickness before seasoning so they cook evenly and achieve proper browning without overcooking the thinner ends.
Let seasoned chicken rest 15-20 minutes at room temperature before breading because salt draws out moisture initially, then reabsorbs for better flavor penetration.
Air fry potatoes at 380°F for 12-15 minutes, shaking halfway through, because this temperature creates the Maillard reaction for browning while the convection removes surface moisture for crispiness.
Chill the sauce for minimum 30 minutes because mayonnaise emulsion tightens as lipids solidify, creating the signature thick consistency that coats food properly.
steps: To make a low calorie cane sauce, in a bowl add light mayo, low sugar ketchup, Worcestershire sauce, salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Mix it all together then set it in the fridge to let it thicken.
Cut the potatoes using a crinkle cutter or just a knife. Add some cooking oil spray and season with chicken stock powder and paprika. Mix it and air fry or oven bake.
Cut the chicken breast into thin strips, season them on each side, and mix it together.
On a plate, add cornflakes with a dash of the same seasonings and crush it into smaller pieces.
Dip each piece of chicken in flour, then the egg, then the cornflakes. Spray them with light cooking oil and air fry or oven bake until golden brown.

heading: Chicken Alfredo
description: Indulge in a creamy dream with our High Protein Chicken Alfredo, packing a whopping 62g of protein per serving! This mouthwatering dish combines tender chicken, rich parmesan, and a velvety sauce that’s perfect for meal prepping. Whip it up quickly and savor the deliciousness all week long!
tips: Cook pasta to only 50-60% doneness (about 4-5 minutes for most shapes) before adding to slow cooker, as it will absorb liquid and finish cooking in the sauce without becoming mushy.
Blend your protein alfredo sauce ingredients for 60-90 seconds minimum to fully break down protein powder particles and prevent grainy texture that occurs with under-blending.
Add lemon juice at the beginning of slow cooking because the acid helps denature chicken proteins at low temperatures (160-180°F), creating more tender, easily shreddable meat.
Reserve 1/2 cup pasta cooking water before draining to add back if the sauce becomes too thick, as the starch content (2-3%) helps bind the protein sauce to pasta.
Let the final dish rest in the slow cooker on warm for 10-15 minutes after combining all ingredients, allowing starches to fully hydrate and sauce to reach optimal thickness.
steps: In your slow cooker, add chicken breast, then squeeze the juice from 1 lemon, followed by the seasonings, diced onion, diced garlic, parmesan cheese, chicken stock, and a tiny bit of pepper. Mix it and let it cook on low or high.
Once the chicken is nearly cooked, start cooking the pasta but only to about halfway.
For the protein alfredo sauce, add all of the ingredients to a blender and blend until smooth and creamy.
If there's any liquid, drain it, then combine the chicken, pasta, and alfredo sauce. Mix well and let it cook for a bit longer to thicken.
Top with more parmesan and parsley, divide into 8 equal servings, and enjoy.

heading: Hawaiian Pizza
description: Indulge in a guilt-free twist on the classic Hawaiian pizza that’s packed with flavor and protein! This scrumptious pie features a whole wheat crust topped with zesty pineapple, lean turkey, and a sprinkle of low-fat mozzarella, bringing a taste of the tropics right to your kitchen. Perfect for a satisfying meal that won't weigh you down, this dish is sure to become a family favorite!
tips: When mixing Greek yogurt dough, work it just until combined - overworking develops too much gluten from the self-rising flour, creating a tough, chewy crust instead of tender.
Pat fresh pineapple chunks completely dry with paper towels before adding to pizza, as excess moisture can create soggy spots and prevent proper browning at 400°F.
Pre-cook turkey bacon to 80% doneness before adding to pizza since the 18-20 minute bake time isn't sufficient to properly render fat and achieve crispness.
Roll the Greek yogurt dough to exactly 1/4-inch thickness - thinner tears easily due to yogurt's moisture content, while thicker won't cook through in the center.
Place pizza on the bottom oven rack for the first 12 minutes, then move to center rack to finish - this ensures the yogurt-based crust sets properly before the top browns.
steps: Mix self-rising flour, salt, garlic powder, nutritional yeast, and Greek yogurt into a tender ball.
Lightly flour a surface and roll out the dough.
Scrunch the edge material for the crust.
Spread marinara, add part-skim mozzarella, turkey bacon, fresh pineapple, and red onions.
Bake in the oven at 400 degrees for 18 to 20 minutes or until the cheese is bubbly.

heading: Honey BBQ Chicken Mac & Cheese
description: Get ready to savor a mouthwatering meal prep delight with our Honey BBQ Chicken Mac & Cheese! This scrumptious dish combines tender, protein-packed chicken glazed in a sweet and tangy honey BBQ sauce, paired with creamy, dreamy mac and cheese that will have you coming back for seconds. Perfect for busy weekdays, this recipe is your ticket to flavorful comfort food that fuels your day!
tips: Turn off heat completely before adding honey to prevent caramelization above 320°F, which creates bitter compounds and causes the honey to seize into hard clumps.
Heat your milk to 140-160°F before adding to cheese to prevent the proteins from shocking and breaking the emulsion, which causes grainy texture.
Add cream cheese first, then cheddar in small handfuls while whisking because cream cheese acts as an emulsifier with 33% fat content, stabilizing the sauce before adding lower-fat cheddar.
Cook pasta 1-2 minutes under package directions since it will continue cooking in the hot cheese sauce, preventing mushy texture during meal prep reheating.
Let assembled portions cool to room temperature before refrigerating to prevent condensation buildup that dilutes the sauce and creates separation during storage.
steps: Cook the chicken on medium high heat for 3-4 minutes on each side until golden brown.
Turn off the heat before adding honey and BBQ sauce, then mix well and set the chicken aside.
In a separate pan, add skimmed milk, hot sauce, light cream cheese, and low fat cheddar cheese on low heat, stirring gently with a whisk until it thickens and is smooth.
Add the cooked macaroni to the sauce and mix well.
Distribute into 4 equal servings and enjoy.

heading: Butter Chicken Lasagna
description: Get ready to indulge in a delicious twist on dinner with our Butter Chicken Lasagna! Layer succulent chicken simmered in rich, spiced tomato sauce with creamy ricotta and gooey mozzarella for a mouthwatering feast. Perfect for meal prep, this high-protein delight will have your taste buds dancing and your fridge stocked for the week ahead!
tips: Toast your garam masala and other whole spices in a dry pan for 30-45 seconds at medium heat before grinding to release volatile oils that increase flavor intensity by 300%.
Temper your heavy cream by adding it slowly to the hot tomato-butter base while whisking to prevent curdling, as the 40°F+ temperature differential will cause proteins to coagulate instantly.
Par-cook your lasagna noodles to exactly 6-7 minutes (2 minutes under package time) so they finish cooking during the 45-minute bake without becoming mushy from double-cooking.
Rest the assembled lasagna for 15-20 minutes before baking to allow noodles to absorb moisture and prevent sauce separation during the high-heat baking process.
Use full-fat ricotta with 15% fat content minimum and drain excess whey for 30 minutes, as low-fat versions release water during baking and create soggy layers.

heading: Chicken Mac and Cheese
