description: Indulge in a bowl of our zesty Chicken Mac and Cheese that’s ready in just 25 minutes! This dish combines tender chicken, creamy cheese sauce, and a kick of spices for a protein-packed meal that's perfect for busy weeknights or meal prepping. Dive into comfort food bliss with just a few simple ingredients and a dash of culinary flair!
ingredients: 1/3 cup light or fat free cream cheese
Salt, pepper, paprika, oregano and garlic powder
tips: Cook chicken to exactly 165°F internal temperature, then let it rest 3 minutes before dicing - this prevents moisture loss that would create dry, rubbery pieces in your creamy sauce.
Heat your milk to 140-150°F before adding to cream cheese to prevent the proteins from seizing and creating a grainy texture instead of smooth emulsion.
Add hot sauce after removing cheese sauce from heat because capsaicin compounds break down at high temperatures, reducing both heat level and flavor complexity.
Reserve 1/2 cup of starchy pasta cooking water before draining - its 2-3% starch content helps bind the cheese sauce to pasta and prevents separation.
Keep cheese sauce temperature below 160°F once formed because higher heat causes proteins to coagulate and fat to separate, breaking your emulsion.
steps: Cook the chicken breast in olive oil, seasoning with salt, pepper, paprika, oregano, and garlic powder.
In a separate pot, combine milk, cream cheese, and low fat cheese over low heat until thickened.
Add hot sauce and cooked macaroni to the cheese sauce, mixing well.
Serve with optional cilantro or parsley on top.

heading: One Pot Hunters Chicken Rice
description: Dive into a mouthwatering experience with our One Pot Hunters Chicken Rice! This cozy dish features tender chicken, crispy bacon, and perfectly cooked rice, all infused with aromatic herbs and spices that will tantalize your taste buds. It’s an effortless meal that brings comfort and flavor to your table in just one pot!
tips: Toast the basmati rice in the rendered chicken fat for 2-3 minutes before adding stock to create a protective starch coating that prevents mushiness and maintains grain separation.
Use a 1.5:1 liquid-to-rice ratio for basmati in one-pot dishes because the grains will continue absorbing moisture from other ingredients during the covered cooking phase.
Cook the rice at exactly 20% power (or lowest simmer) once covered because higher heat creates steam pockets that cook unevenly and make rice gummy.
Let the dish rest off-heat for 5 minutes before fluffing because residual steam finishes cooking the top layer and prevents broken grains when stirring.
Position your oven rack 6 inches from the broiler element so cheese melts completely before the surface burns, achieving optimal browning in 3-4 minutes.
steps: Fry off your chicken, bacon and the seasoning in a large frying pan.
Once browned add in your onions and cook until soft, then add in the garlic, tomato purée and bbq sauce and cook for a few minutes.
Add in the uncooked basmati and the chicken stock.
Pop the lid on and cook on a low heat for around 20 minutes or until the rice is fully cooked, adding more liquid if needed.
Make sure you don’t stir the rice after it’s cooked, just fluff it with a fork.
Once cooked sprinkle with the cheese, place it under the grill until golden and bubbling.
Drizzle over the bbq sauce. Serve and enjoy.

heading: Honey Garlic Paneer
description: Indulge in the delightful fusion of sweet and savory with our Honey Garlic Paneer! This dish brings together succulent paneer, fragrant garlic, and a drizzle of golden honey for a burst of flavor in every bite. With just a handful of ingredients, you’ll whip up a protein-packed meal that's both nutritious and irresistibly tasty!
tips: Fry paneer at 300-325°F to create a golden crust without overcooking the interior, as paneer's protein structure begins to toughen above 350°F.
Bloom the garlic for exactly 30-45 seconds before adding liquids to activate allicin compounds without burning, which turns bitter above 280°F.
Use a 2:1 ratio of cornflour to honey when coating vegetables to ensure proper thickening without overpowering sweetness - the cornflour gelatinizes at 185°F creating the glossy sauce.
Add paneer back to the pan during the last 2-3 minutes only, as extended cooking causes paneer to release whey and become rubbery due to excessive protein coagulation.
Keep sauce temperature between 180-200°F during the final simmer to maintain honey's flavor compounds while allowing cornflour to fully hydrate and thicken.
steps: Take 5 g oil and fry paneer in it.
In the same pan take garlic, spring onions, 1 tsp soya sauce, 1 tsp green chilli sauce and 100 ml water mix it on low flame for 2 mins.
Mix onion and capsicum with 20 g corn flour and 10 g honey.
Cook for 5 mins.
Add the roasted paneer.
Cook for 5 more mins.
Garnish with more spring onions and enjoy!

heading: Paneer Sandwich
description: Craving a delicious and nutritious bite? Whip up this easy paneer sandwich packed with protein! Featuring creamy paneer, zesty spices, and fresh veggies, this delightful creation comes together in no time for a satisfying snack or quick lunch. Enjoy a burst of flavor with every bite!
tips: Cook paneer at medium heat (275-300°F) for no more than 2-3 minutes to prevent the proteins from toughening and becoming rubbery.
Salt the vegetables first and let them sweat for 30 seconds before adding paneer, as this draws out moisture and prevents the filling from making your bread soggy.
Pat paneer dry with paper towels before cooking to remove surface moisture, which can reduce browning and create steam instead of the desired sear.
Toast the bread at 375°F for 3-4 minutes to create a moisture barrier through the Maillard reaction, preventing the filling from penetrating and softening the bread.
Cut vegetables to 1/8-inch thickness so they cook in the same 2-3 minute timeframe as the paneer, ensuring even doneness without overcooking the protein.
steps: Add ginger garlic paste, onion, and capsicum, cut into thin slices, to a pan.
Add salt and Szechuan sauce, then add 150 grams of low-fat paneer.
Once cooked, spread the mixture on bread and toast it.

heading: McNuggets
description: Get ready to indulge guilt-free with these Easy Low Calorie Spicy McNuggets! Packed with protein and made with zesty seasonings and crispy breadcrumbs, these nuggets are a delicious twist on a fast-food classic, but with half the calories. Perfect for satisfying your cravings while keeping your macros in check!
tips: Freeze the formed nuggets for exactly 10-15 minutes to firm the protein structure, preventing them from falling apart during coating and cooking while avoiding ice crystal formation that occurs after 20+ minutes.
Keep your hands at 15-20% moisture when forming nuggets by dipping in cold water - this prevents the lean chicken mince from sticking while maintaining proper binding without adding excess moisture.
Add 1/2 teaspoon baking powder per cup of flour to create CO2 bubbles during cooking, giving you 30% more surface area for crispiness without traditional deep-frying.
Air fry at 380°F for optimal Maillard reaction - this temperature creates browning and flavor development in the coating while cooking the interior to 165°F without drying out the lean mince.
Form nuggets to 1-inch thickness maximum because chicken mince has less fat than whole meat and will overcook on the exterior before the center reaches 165°F if thicker.
steps: In a bowl, add chicken mince followed by the seasonings listed.
Mix it together, make sure your hands are slightly wet and form 24 even nuggets.
Place them on a baking tray with baking paper and freeze for around 10 minutes.
In a bowl, add plain flour, baking powder, a tiny bit of pepper, and a dash of the same seasonings used before. Mix it all together.
Dip each piece of chicken in egg and coat it in the mixture.
Spray with light cooking oil spray and air fry or oven bake them with the specified settings.

heading: Pepper Mayo Sliders
description: Savor the ultimate guilt-free treat with our zesty KFC-inspired Pepper Mayo Sliders! Packed with high protein and low calories, these mouthwatering bites feature juicy chicken seasoned to perfection and a creamy, spicy mayo that takes it to the next level. Perfect for a quick meal or a fun gathering, these sliders are sure to be a hit!
ingredients: 475g Raw Chicken Breast (cut into 5 pieces - 95g each)
tips: Bake your cornflake-crusted chicken at 400°F for 12-15 minutes to achieve optimal Maillard browning while preventing the cornflakes from burning due to their low moisture content.
Crush cornflakes to 1/4-inch pieces maximum because smaller fragments create better adhesion to the egg wash while larger pieces fall off during cooking.
Use Greek yogurt at 2% fat minimum for flatbread dough because its protein content (10g per 100g) creates better gluten development and chewier texture than regular yogurt.
Cook flatbreads on medium heat (325-350°F surface temp) for exactly 2 minutes per side to develop steam pockets without over-browning the exterior.
Add lemon juice to mayo at a 10:1 ratio (mayo to lemon) because the acid breaks down proteins and creates a smoother emulsion while brightening the pepper flavor.
steps: Start by grabbing cornflakes, and for the KFC secret herbs and spices, add a dash of the seasonings listed, then crush it into smaller pieces.
Cut your chicken into 5 equal strips, then dip it in beaten egg, and coat it in the crushed cornflakes.
Add some cooking spray, then bake it in the oven or air fryer at the specified settings.
For the KFC pepper mayo, mix together light mayo, lemon juice, and pepper.
You can use store-bought flatbread or make your own by mixing flour, baking powder, salt, and yogurt.
Mix it, knead it to form a dough ball, then roll it out on a floured surface and cut out 5 small flatbreads.
Cook them on medium heat for 2 minutes on each side.
Assemble each slider and enjoy!

heading: Fried Rice & Honey Chipotle Chicken
description: Savor the deliciousness of our Honey Chipotle Chicken Fried Rice, a guilt-free delight packed with protein! This vibrant dish features tender chicken marinated in a sweet and smoky honey chipotle glaze, tossed with fluffy rice and colorful veggies for a satisfying meal that won't derail your wellness goals. Dive into this flavorful fusion that proves healthy eating can be utterly delicious!
tips: Use day-old rice that's been refrigerated because the starch molecules retrograde and firm up, preventing mushy fried rice and creating distinct, separated grains.
Cook chicken pieces in a single layer without overcrowding to achieve proper Maillard browning at 300-350°F surface temperature rather than steaming.
Add eggs to the pan first and let them set for 30-45 seconds before scrambling to create larger, silkier curds that won't break apart when mixed with rice.
Heat your wok or pan to smoking point (around 400°F) before adding ingredients because high heat creates wok hei - the smoky flavor from rapid moisture evaporation.
Add frozen vegetables directly to the hot pan without thawing because their ice crystals create steam that helps them cook evenly while maintaining texture.
steps: Start out with boneless skinless chicken breast and cut them into small thin cubes.
In a bowl, marinate the chicken with soy sauce, brown sugar, and a bunch of spices.
For the glaze, mix chipotle adobo, honey, and minced garlic.
Quickly mix an egg with some egg whites and add a bit of salt for the egg mixture.
In a medium heat pan, cook the chicken until it browns on each side, then add the glaze.
In another medium heat pan, add minced garlic and frozen peas and carrots.
Add the eggs and day-old jasmine rice, along with a bit more soy sauce, sesame oil, and sriracha.

heading: Cajun Chicken Pasta
description: Savor the rich flavors of our Creamy Cajun Chicken Pasta, a mouthwatering meal prep option that packs a punch with 62 grams of protein—all for just 500 calories! Featuring tender chicken, zesty Cajun spices, and a luscious cream sauce, this dish brings excitement to your weekly menu. Whip it up in no time and enjoy a satisfying, guilt-free feast!
tips: Air fry chicken at exactly 375°F for 15-20 minutes to achieve the Maillard reaction while retaining 85% moisture content, creating a crispy exterior without drying out the protein.
Blend the cottage cheese sauce for 60-90 seconds until completely smooth because cottage cheese curds need extended blending to break down protein structures and create a silky texture.
Use a 3:1 ratio of cottage cheese to parmesan (1.5 cups to 0.5 cups) because cottage cheese provides creaminess while parmesan adds umami depth without overpowering the Cajun spices.
Reserve 1/2 cup pasta cooking water before draining because the starchy, salted water helps the cottage cheese sauce adhere to pasta and prevents separation.
Add tomato paste to the sauce blend because it contains glutamates that amplify the savory flavor of Cajun seasoning by 25-30% while adding natural acidity to balance the cottage cheese.
steps: Combine one and a half cups of cottage cheese, half a cup of grated parmesan, tablespoon of Cajun seasoning, two garlic cloves, tablespoon of tomato paste, and half a cup of fat-free milk in a blender and blend until smooth.
Season 24 ounces of chicken breast with garlic salt and Cajun seasoning until fully coated and air fry at 375 for 15 to 20 minutes.
Add cooked protein pasta to a bowl along with the sauce, cooked bell peppers, and onions and mix until fully coated.
Top the pasta with chicken, fresh chives, and red pepper flakes.

heading: Pasta
description: Dive into this vibrant street-style spicy pasta that packs a powerful punch with 33 grams of protein! Featuring a tantalizing blend of zesty cherry tomatoes and fiery red pepper flakes, this dish is not only delicious but also comes in at just 530 calories. Perfect for a quick weeknight dinner that’ll leave your taste buds dancing!
tips: Boil soy chunks at a rolling 212°F for exactly 8-10 minutes to achieve optimal protein extraction - longer cooking breaks down amino acid chains and reduces bioavailability by up to 15%.
Grind boiled soy chunks to 3-4mm pieces using pulse action rather than continuous grinding to maintain fibrous texture that mimics meat without creating paste.
Cook whole wheat pasta to 85% doneness (2 minutes under package time) since it will finish cooking in the sauce and absorb flavors while maintaining al dente texture.
Reduce tomato-spice mixture until moisture content drops to 15-20% (sauce coats spoon without dripping) to prevent pasta from becoming soggy when combined.
Reserve 1/2 cup starchy pasta water before draining - its 2-3% starch content acts as a natural emulsifier to bind the sauce to pasta proteins.
steps: First, boil the soy chunks and grind them coarsely.
Sauté the onion in a little oil for 2-3 minutes.
Add the tomato and green chili paste along with the spices shown on the screen.
Cook for 4-5 minutes until reduced.
Add the whole wheat pasta and mix well, cooking until everything is combined.
